text.title: I Investigated the Most Crowded Island on Earth 🇰🇪
text.u: On the outskirts of Kena lies an island.
The most densely populated island on
Earth with 500 people living on an
island that's the size of half a
football pitch. This is Mingo Island and
it's a crazy place. It is going to be a
bit of a process to get to this island
where we are going to be spending the
night. Got a couple matus to catch. We
got a boat ride to catch. We're going to
have to bribe people get fishermanmen to
take us to this island. And uh I think
the journey starts all here in this bus
Let's see what happens. First of all,
the only person enough to join me on
>> Are you ready? Are you ready to stay the
>> Right. Where are we going? We're going
>> Yeah. So, we need to go to a place
I trust I trust this guy.
>> I don't know what we mean.
>> Do they have to wait till it's full?
Yeah, they wait till it's full. So, we
could actually still be here another
like hour or so. Okay. This is not going
>> now. You want money or after?
>> Okay. Please. I would like that. Thank
>> Yeah. Wait, why don't you put your bag
down under the seat like a normal
>> Yeah, there's so much random stuff being
sold. This lady selling uh shirts, these
guys selling socks, everything. Got men
>> Anyways, you can buy anything down
African bus station. That's like a real
guys. The music man. What is this guy?
>> Wait, how much is the total?
>> Are you This one is This one.
>> Are you Are you the driver?
>> All right. You can keep that.
>> You always want to get the driver on
>> So, if there's any arguments or any
things that happen on the bus journey,
>> you're going to be sweet. So,
>> he may just not be the driver.
>> Anyway, guys, this island that we're
visiting, Mingo Island, an island that
has been disputed between Uganda and
Kenya, it lies on Lake Victoria. In
2008, there was a dispute between Kenya
and Uganda about who actually owned this
island. And they both stationed military
officers on the island. And in 2009,
indicating that it was 500 m east of the
Victoria, making it part of Kenya. So
that's where we are today. Presently,
there's around 500 people that live on
the island. You got Ugandans, you got
Kenyans, you got also Tanzanians living
casino, and bars. We're going to try out
all of those. And there's about to be a
bogan Australian living on the island as
>> I wonder if I will be the first
>> Yeah. I'm going to take a [ __ ] on it.
Mark my territory. God, we're going to
sp end up spending like 3 hours in
there. So, we may as well get some fresh
air. But essentially, these are called
patos. They are little buses. You got 14
passengers and they fill up once they
are all booked out, all the seats. So,
we have around six people. Jumbo, how
>> I'm fine. I'm not trying to cuz I've
been injured. Somebody beat me here.
>> Anyways, that's a a bus station here in
Kisamu. We should probably get back
inside because the moment that we stood
out here, [ __ ] went to uh [ __ ] happened.
as soon as I hop out, the wholeing
>> I just saw someone punch in. What even
>> guys? I think we actually have a full a
>> we're getting there. Yeah, we're good,
>> Hey, maybe the ticket man is coming.
>> So, this is the driver. This is the
>> We gave him an extra 100 booster. The
>> The back seat is not ideal. It's always
>> I like it. I like it. All
everybody. We've been there for about 2
>> All right, that's our stretch done. So,
the matus, they just they stop off
about four times. There's a few people
that are uh picking up different items
>> We're stopped off in a random town.
>> 240 for what? For what? I I I I'll go to
>> Why? Why? Why? Why? Why? Why? Why?
>> He's looking He's dressed so well.
He has money. He has money. I don't have
I got to make sure the
>> Yeah. Just like in one one or like one
You take this or you take this one
>> Thank you. Thank you. We got a chapati
everybody. I don't know what you mix it
Now obviously you intermix this thing
with other food or curry or
other things, but personally I love
>> I was really enjoying this ride.
>> It was so good. I am still enjoying it,
but little less comfortable. But it is
>> The issue is every time we go over a
bump, I hit my head on the on the middle
>> Oh my god. We got the VIP spot.
>> what is your name? My name is
>> Can you get us to Sorry. Sorry.
David driving. Or maybe not. Maybe
We may not even make it to the island
>> Yeah, we That took way too long.
>> Me and Carol are now officially married,
>> so Kieran has found a gizer that might
>> I don't even know he has a boat.
>> There's no way we can trust this guy.
>> Yeah, we probably shouldn't go with this
guy. All right, we're in the dock area,
everybody. This is where all the
fishermen come and put their fish. So,
these are all getting dried up. So,
these are actually the fish nets. And
look, got the little uh little side. It
smells absolutely putrid here. I hate
>> But if we're going to find someone to
>> where we need to go, it's going to be
>> Nice to meet you. Me and my friend, we
want to go to the island. You know,
>> Not now. If you might come in the
morning, you might find a lift. But
>> right now, people want to go to fish,
>> How long does it take to get to
>> Uh, about uh two 2 to 3 hours.
>> bro. It takes like 2 to 3 hours to get
>> Well, I mean, I I trust the fisherman
more than me. So, what time do you guys
>> No, but they normally fish at night.
>> And they they normally spend the night
>> Yeah, you got your phone. Yeah. Thank
>> That is my WhatsApp. So, what do you
guys catch? What fish is it in there?
>> We don't want to call them.
>> And these are the nets that you catch
>> So, it goes from like here and then you
>> Yeah. So the challenges that we have
here when there is rain they didn't they
won't dry up. So that is the main
>> Yeah. Yeah. And how much is 1 kg 1 kilo?
>> They are normally nice. Have you taste
>> Not yet. I don't like fish.
>> You don't like Oh, you're vegetarian.
>> So, are there police on the island,
>> You don't have to like pay fine or
>> Uh, kuna. There is no like that. Yeah.
>> So, how much would you charge to go
tomorrow? What and what time? How much
>> Really? The other guy wanted to charge
>> 15. You shall pay 3,000 for fuel, then a
>> Which boat? Which boat is it?
>> Yeah, we we have several boats, so one
>> All right. Do you think he's
>> We don't really have a choice, do we?
>> He seemed all right to be fair.
>> He gave us a lot cheaper than the other
>> Yeah. What the hell was that? 15.
>> Jamo. Hey. Well, we found the nicest
hotel in the city, everybody. And we're
going to try and get a night and wake up
go to the island toilet. It looks
Window's broken. When was the last guest
>> Okay. Do you have two bed ones?
>> You want a single with two bed?
>> In this room, what is the problem?
That's why we took you to the other
>> How much is it per night?
>> 2,000. I kind of want to shower. Yeah.
It's either we have a shower and sleep
in the same bed or don't sleep in the
same bed and don't have a shower.
>> from the top or the bottom?
>> We just have to wait for some minutes.
Then the water will just come.
>> Yeah, that's that's fine. Just sit down.
>> All right. Sold. We'll take it. One,
Does this have potential to be the worst
>> No, this does not take the number one
spot, but it is extremely run down.
>> This is actually all right to be fair.
>> At least the sheets are clean.
>> Yeah, that the first one was
>> Look at how many even on there.
Definitely need to have the now.
>> If you got too big. I haven't unlocked
one yet. You got to do more side quests
until you unlock it. What do I have? Oh,
Well, I think I have less mosquitoes
than Kieran, which is good. Definitely
up here. So, I'm most certainly going to
or a fan. I'm going to take this
outside. I was going to do a room tour.
This is the room. Mosquito net. Open
windows. Lots of mosquitoes around. No
fan. My shower doesn't work, so I have
to shower in Kieran's room. Is there a
It actually is a towel and a balcony.
But we do have a lake view, which is
So, before we cut to tomorrow and the
adventures of getting to the island, we
are going to do a cigarette review.
Everybody, my favorite thing on this
channel, as you all know, we have TE's
and C's of the cigarettes and my
I'm only allowed to smoke when I'm
inside of the Middle East. But there are
loopholes to the T's and C's, and those
include for every country I go to, I
need to try the local cigarettes. So
these are super match. They got a
football player on the side. These were
These are going to remind me of uh like
Winnie Blues in Australia or like
Not actually too bad. I was expecting it
to be so tar. I honestly haven't smoked
cigarettes for a hot minute. So, the
first one back is always terrible.
It's actually quite pleasant. Not tarry,
not strong. It's got a nice pull
cigarettes that I could take over to the
island. The people will be happy. Hello.
We found the only restaurant in this
There's no way they have pizza. No, full
chicken. I might get a [ __ ] full
chicken. See, the thing with these
places is that they have the menu, but
like everything's always going to be
sold out. They're never going to
actually have it. Do you have pizza?
>> Oh, no cheese. That's okay. Honestly,
this is just too embarrassing to vlog. I
can't hold myself eating this egg and
>> All right, guys. I figured out the
night's sleep on the island won't be too
bad. I had a shower. I got everything
done. And I felt like an Indian washing.
That's how we used to uh have showers in
India using the bucket. But going to get
some sleep and then tomorrow morning
we're going to make our way on the boat.
getting in. We've been bitten like
crazy. We killed one there. Blood stains
Well, good morning, guys. Boy, oh boy.
Did not get much sleep. I had a freaking
bird come into the room in the middle of
the night at like 1:00 a.m. What the
[ __ ] is that? Is that a [ __ ] bird or
How do you even get in here?
What the [ __ ] are you doing, bro? In
about 30 minutes, we have planned with
Sylvester to take us to Mingo Island.
>> Terribly. My mattress is covering blood
>> Really? Oh my god. I have squashed a
>> Yeah, there's even loads inside now
>> Dude, I don't know if it's just me, but
I swear mosquito nets never actually
work. Like somehow they always end up
inside. Yeah, I woke up three separate
times to that horrible buzzing and I was
like, "Oh, maybe they're just on the
outside." And then I actually put my
flashlight and there was loads. So, I
spent like 10 minutes killing them. Oh,
also my toilet um has a hole in so I
went for a piss and I forgot to flush
and now my floor is covered in piss.
>> Oh, yeah. I didn't want to say it when I
walked in, but it smells like a urinal.
>> Yeah. Um and I had to shower with that
bucket. But other than that, perfect.
>> Ready to go to the island. First thing
is this is going to be luxury compared
>> I reckon tonight will be all right.
>> It won't. We're sleeping in a metal
shack in the middle of a lake that's
>> You have uh mosquito spray, right?
>> Yeah, but it only works in Bali. It's
only that pink one. It doesn't work
>> It's not built against the African
>> for two. How much? 50 50 two people.
All right. I bet you're loving this
All right. We're off to an Oreo sandwich
Your white is funny in the lower half.
All right. So, 50 uh Kenya chilies.
Thank you. What the [ __ ] was that?
Okay, thank you. I fair enough,
honestly. Thank you. All right, we are
>> Jumbo Sylvester is waiting for us
somewhere down there. So, we go see him.
>> Very good. Would you like your friend?
>> It's okay. We have our friend.
>> So, you ready to go on an adventure?
>> Never. It's never easy here in Kenya.
>> Got to uh always do more things. So, we
have to go speak to speak to somebody.
Yeah, we must speak to somebody because
we we supposed to record to the
>> For that so that I can bring food so
that we won't run out of
Feel like this is how people get
three airlines. So really small one then
>> So the big one no man live there.
>> Why? A small small island Sasa they are
>> so do you go there often?
>> So the bigger one no one can live there
it's is just like the building here
>> All right. Sylvester's got the gas
to go and come with you take but because
we are going and coming back.
>> If you can pay us for staying there,
>> we can stay with you all over the day,
>> So, how much will that be?
>> I don't know. I don't know cuz there is
>> where we can spend the night.
>> We shall pay everything there. We we
won't let you to pay because you shall
pay much. So, we we we we are the one
>> all those things. So before we enter at
midingo the mean migingo there are some
>> So there we we are the one who shall pay
>> So we won't have to pay bribe like you
won't pay. So long as you agree with the
boy everything is now okay. So there's
>> even if they see that we're mongu maybe
>> we are the one who shall solve all the
>> okay we want to go like now
>> and then tomorrow maybe like 6:00 or
>> Let me uh speak with my business
>> Yes. A lot of uh shillings.
>> Yeah. I hope not. So long as you step
>> You won't give out any amount of money.
>> Let me speak with my uh my associate.
>> Get it down to to 30. That's on the
condition that they there's no extra fee
>> Yeah. The issue is I don't have that
much shillings. They only have it in
>> cuz someone decided not to get [ __ ]
>> we've already paid 3K in fuel. So, we
>> Yeah, but what if the What about the
>> For the fuel. So, we are going to add
>> We are going to add more anything. You
>> Yeah, guest house. Guest house. You will
>> We don't eat fish. So, we'll probably
>> So, that one we have to organize.
>> Let's go to the office. Then we take our
>> Investor was calling us here to the
office. So we are going to like leaving.
>> [ __ ] is going this is like a government
>> Can I give you my sister?
>> yeah, I'm getting sold his sister.
>> So I'll give you my sister.
>> All right. All right. How old is she?
>> 21. Oh, nice. This is my my future wife.
>> Kira. Thank you. Welcome. So, we meet.
>> I've been told by my friend my colleague
that you want to move to island.
>> Yeah. Just for one day just to see
>> What is there is to give you a concept
better so that when you do that from
>> the canyon side you have pass on this
>> Let's figure out the price. This is a
>> What are we going to agree on?
>> Yeah. So, assist me with a certain
dollar so that I can change it there
>> Okay. But we can pay for the fuel in
advance, but we don't want to pay the
whole amount now because we don't know
you. You guys I'm not saying you will,
but you guys could just go.
>> We're paying this much to have the worst
Why are we putting oursel through this?
We're paying $270 to go and sleep on an
>> We can go back to the Marit or Swahili
>> We're getting our fuel done over there.
>> So the thing with Africa is that
everything just takes so long. You
It's taken us an hour since meeting uh
the guys down by the beach to still
haven't even fully agreed the price.
>> We're just having to cop it.
>> I don't know. So, yeah. Apparently, it
takes 3 hours to get there. We'll see.
Maybe we'll see. We're finally ready to
go, everybody. Walking through the uh
fisherman's village or where they do all
so this is our boat, I think.
>> Wow, look at all the green algae and
everything, but all these boats, uh,
fisherman boats, they go around the
>> For deposit can give him like 20.
>> How much do you think $100 bill?
>> Why? What does he need it for? Yeah, you
>> It's all good. Good to go.
>> Mr. Mr. So to me I'm remaining here.
>> Yeah. See you. We see you when we come
>> Can I get even a bottle of soda, my
>> We'll buy it for you when we come back.
Okay. All right. We're hopping into the
>> We're finally making our way to Migo
Island. What a journey. Now's even
started. A real journey. What happened
on this island with like Uganda and
Kenya? They're fighting for the island
>> Yeah. They don't fight but before they
used to fight. Nowadays they don't fight
because there are Kenyan borders patrol
they are there Ugandan border patrol
there they unite people and solve the
problem is the reason why those border
patrol are there they are coast guards
>> and there are also from Uganda they are
there to solve any case that other
people want to bring chaos police they
shall take them to to court or so that
they can bring down Okay. And but it is
in Kenyan territory like Kenya owns the
>> Before they were you guys you guys were
complaining that that the island were
yours that was theirs. But right now it
was confirmed that the island is Kenya.
>> The Kenyans own the island. Yeah, that
>> yeah the weather is too much wind the
wind too many pull nothing so that it
>> How do you think the sleeping conditions
will be tonight? I've seen some videos
of people that have stayed here in the
past. It doesn't look as bad as I think
it's going to be, but I think the
mosquitoes are going to be the the
>> Yeah, we're probably going to get
malaria after this like Victoria before
>> I did. Look, it is uh preventable and
curable. You can get cured if you have
>> We're going to be indeed the poorest
country on the planet. So, I can't
imagine healthare is going to be the
So these people on the boats here, they
actually live out here. They cook on the
boat, they sleep on the boat, and they
>> How often do they go back to land?
>> Every 2 weeks. Imagine that's your life.
>> We're living on a boat out here with
>> This is Okay. You keep it.
>> So, we've been given back the 2,000. We
We need 2k back so that we can then give
them the security, the border. Make sure
that they leave us alone. So, it's like
the bribe. You know, here you can bribe
your way into a lot of things, including
not getting hassled whilst you're in
>> Yeah. That's your number. What's her
>> I love you. She's actually kind of hot.
>> very nice to meet you. So, I got two
So, we've been going for about an hour
now. The whole time I thought that that
was the main island that you like get
the police stuff from and then behind it
is big and go because the way that it's
shown on maps is that if you're on the
mainland, that island covers it. But
turns out that is just a random island
that is nowhere near where we're
>> Yeah, it's a 3-hour boat ride. There's
>> Yeah. So, it's the next island that
we're going to. It's all the way in the
So, why is nobody staying on that
>> Can you Can you build the house there?
>> Well, I don't know. It looks more
habitable than the rock that we're going
to. Oh, dude. There's the first look of
it. Wow. That's actually sick. Is crazy.
>> So, our first point of call is up there,
>> We got to check in with the border
police, the patrol, give them the 2k
>> mate. Out of all the islands that they
have, people are living perfectly fine
choose the rock. Rock in the middle of
the ocean. All right. So, this is how we
>> We made it to the camp.
>> This what I'm asking you. Are you a co-
>> Do you have a letter permitted by the
>> to carry people or to f people over the
>> So last time was March. Okay. So today
10. So do I have to give him the 2K?
>> Yeah. Nice. He was a nice guy. But we
got permission to be here and film
everybody after a uh a little bit of uh
>> He's regarded as a territory. But the
is still being demarcated. There's a
>> Because when we talk the we talk about
>> So the two countries are still in the
>> Yeah. So you will get the two regime
>> Uganda there and some Kenyan police.
>> So if you are here you are in Kenya.
>> Why do people choose that island instead
>> They choose that land because here they
>> As you can see waves comes from that
>> So those that vessels can't dock here.
>> So they they decided to stay on the
>> Imagine trying to sleep with these as
>> It's a good place. Just come.
>> We gave the uh the Cokes to the guys.
They're very appreciative of it. But now
we got to get our boat to dock in the
And then we got to go over there to
where we're staying tonight. Got to deal
with a few other government procedures,
we like to say, on the Ugandan side.
And then we'll be free to roam. Man, it
>> Half the size of a football pitch.
>> And so how much do we have to give
>> I think I think it's about 3,000.
>> Let's go. It is kind of sketchy.
Okay, we're clear. We're good. Oh my
god, bro. How do these rocks not like
officially making Oh, mate, what are you
trying to do? Get an anchor up your
Oh, that's the worst. That's what ever
>> I got ankle deep in water. All right,
>> Oh, look at all the fish right here.
>> has anybody heard of democracy? We are
here to make this a democratic nation. I
think we're meeting with the Ug.
>> So, it's all built on a rock.
>> Are these your your friends? You know
>> Wow. Look at this economy, guys. It's
booming. You got even like stone paths
>> Very much like a small one.
>> But you can just see the extent of where
how many people live here and how many
homes there are. All made out of tin
shacks. You got little corner shops.
They got eggs. That's good. All right,
we've lost Kieran and everyone, but the
there's not a whole lot to uh
It's not that difficult to find people
into a little bit of trouble filming. We
lost lost Kieran somehow. We uh we lost
each other. Then we found ourselves in
the Ugandan side. This place is crazy.
>> Very nice. Very nice. All right, we're
going down the side, John. Now we're
Now we're done with the room.
Yeah. Can we see the room? Yeah.
>> Now you can start with this. Look at it.
>> There is one there and another one
>> All right. Which one's the best one?
>> Which one has the best view?
>> The best view? I think this one.
>> All right. I see that. Yeah.
>> Okay. There's this. Is it? Oh, nice.
>> Let me get a light so I can see.
>> Yeah. How much is it for night?
>> How much is that a night?
>> And where will the boys sleep? The boys,
I'm going to take them to Okay, wait.
>> So, this is my room, everybody. For $125
per night. This is it. We're a tin
shack. We got all the bugs and
everything. Yeah, there's lots of bugs
I don't know. At least I have a a duvet
actually kind of comfy. It's just the
bugs that I'm worried about. Look up
>> Hello, MTV and welcome to my crib.
>> You've managed to rent yourself a double
>> I was actually thinking the next one,
even though it's a single bed, the next
one's over. They don't smell like piss.
>> That just means that people are pissing
We can swap if you want. I don't
>> I also have LTE in here. So, I feel like
>> So, apparently we also have a police
officer that's sleeping with us tonight.
And he's going to be sharing Kieran's
>> I think he's here for security. So, the
Kenyan side have been generous to uh
gift us one of their officers. He's
going to be sleeping in there. The
reason for that is they said that
there's been abductions in not on this
But we got Mate, you can't complain
>> I'm not complaining, man. I'm actually
>> This is uh you pay $100 in other parts
of the world for this view.
Look at that beauty. But it does just
>> But look, you could even do a little bit
We need to do an introduction because
when we got off the boat, we met this
>> Lean. And uh how do you know him?
>> I'm just knowing right here. We have
just came together from the Milan.
>> Anyway, he's he's been helpful in uh
different people. And what is the name
of this guest house that we're in?
>> Oh, this is our security. Oh, from the
Kenyon side. So, he's sleeping in the
>> Very good. Very good. All right. Where
while I'm going to organize stuff,
>> dude. It's actually insane. Look at all
the bugs. They're in your hair.
>> Yeah. Why Why are they here?
>> Because they are from the the the
There's nowhere else for them to go.
>> Yeah. Here's the our praying place.
>> We are Muslim. Here is my fellow Muslim.
>> How long have you lived here for?
>> We have been living here for around
>> 20 years. And how old are you?
>> Oh, so all your life basically.
>> Yeah. But Muslims are very many
>> they some have went to their jobs. Some
>> So you do the call to prayer in the
>> We do pray five times a day as you know.
>> So when was this mosque built? This
small since the island has started.
>> This one is around one year.
>> One year. It was very small.
>> Okay. So now it's bigger. Expanded
>> And how old is the island? Like when did
at at night time do they stay here the
flies or they go away? The flies at
night they are not they are not very
>> Yeah. So it's not usually like this
>> They just love there is a light in my
>> Now you know right now it's their
>> You know they come from Uganda that way.
>> And then they land here. They stay for a
period of time and then they
>> Wait. So this is for the guest house or
>> Yeah. For for all we Muslims.
>> For everyone. Yeah. We can use.
>> And where does it go outside? Yeah,
>> This is such an interesting place,
>> bro. So, what is life like here?
>> Now, the life here depends on the
availability of the fish. M so there is
no fish it is a big issue here.
>> Yeah. When there is a scarce of fish
>> now the life becomes difficult because
>> And sometimes in the year there is less
>> Now the population here they go back to
three months they are good. December it
reduces in number. Now the energy
>> Wow. What do you do for work on the
island? You were fishing as well.
>> Now I I have my I I'm just the manager.
>> Same company. Now those are fisherman
>> As you can see, you know, so I work with
the National Youth Service in Kenya.
That is the tour guide. Yeah.
>> I just text my documents to the Kenya
>> Yeah. So, what country are you from?
>> And were you born here or you born
>> So why did why did your family decide to
>> Because my father has been here in
invested in this waterow. Now after I I
I am I was done with my my secondary
level. Now I just decided to help him as
>> Like today you see how the weather is
>> Now we might face some difficulties in
terms of charging our batteries our
>> Is there only solar? There's not any
>> A generator. Now the generator might
>> What is he doing with it here?
>> This is the fuel. Now the he supplies to
>> yeah for the for the engine to be driven
Now this is the fuel the the machine is
going to use it when they are going for
the to look for the fish. Wow.
>> On the lake that about that that
mountain there is no any other there's
>> the the mountain is somehow sloping.
>> Yeah. Now not suitable for want to live
there and also it is said to be there
>> Snakes compared to that one also have
some snakes but not that much. the
place whenever they migrate they just
decide to go there because here they
>> So do do people ever go there?
>> Yeah. Just just like visitors when they
come they usually like to visit there to
